concepts.name: Memory Wall
concepts.description: The architectural bottleneck where moving data costs significantly more time and energy than performing arithmetic calculations.

concepts.name: Hardware-Software Co-design
concepts.description: The design principle where algorithms are tailored to hardware capabilities and hardware is built around specific algorithmic requirements.

concepts.name: Amdahl's Law
concepts.description: The mathematical principle defining the maximum possible speedup from hardware acceleration based on the non-parallelizable fraction of a workload.

concepts.name: Arithmetic Intensity
concepts.description: The ratio of floating-point operations performed to bytes of memory traffic required during execution.

concepts.name: Roofline Model
concepts.description: An analytical framework that diagnoses whether a workload is compute-bound or memory-bound based on its arithmetic intensity and hardware limits.

concepts.name: Integration Bottleneck
concepts.description: The primary performance limit in modern AI caused by the latency and energy cost of moving massive data to highly parallel compute units.

concepts.name: Processing Elements
concepts.description: The fundamental building blocks of AI accelerators that integrate dedicated computational units and local memory.

concepts.name: Vector Operations
concepts.description: Computations that apply a single mathematical instruction to multiple independent data elements simultaneously.

concepts.name: Matrix Operations
concepts.description: Structured, multi-dimensional mathematical transformations that dominate the execution time of neural networks.

concepts.name: Special Function Units
concepts.description: Dedicated hardware circuits designed to execute non-linear mathematical operations like activations and normalizations efficiently.

concepts.name: SIMD Execution
concepts.description: A hardware execution model that applies identical operations to multiple data elements in parallel to maximize throughput.

concepts.name: SIMT Execution
concepts.description: A hardware execution model that scales parallel processing across thousands of independent threads sharing instruction execution.

concepts.name: Tensor Cores
concepts.description: Specialized hardware units optimized for executing multi-dimensional matrix multiplications and accumulations on fixed-size blocks.

concepts.name: Structured Sparsity
concepts.description: A predictable pattern of zero values that allows hardware accelerators to efficiently compress and compute sparse matrices without irregular memory access penalties.

concepts.name: Systolic Arrays
concepts.description: A grid architecture where data streams synchronously between neighboring processing elements to maximize operand reuse without external memory access.

concepts.name: The Tiling Principle
concepts.description: The strategy of partitioning large tensor computations into smaller blocks that fit exactly into an accelerator's fast local memory.

concepts.name: Mixed-Precision Computing
concepts.description: The strategic use of different numerical formats throughout an execution pipeline to optimize speed and memory bandwidth while preserving accuracy.

concepts.name: Bandwidth Taper
concepts.description: The physical constraint where data transfer speeds progressively decrease at each hierarchical level moving outward from the compute units.

concepts.name: im2col Transformation
concepts.description: An algorithmic technique that reshapes convolutional operations into standard matrix multiplications for efficient hardware execution.

concepts.name: Computation Placement
concepts.description: The spatial assignment of specific operations to individual processing elements to maximize parallelism and minimize idle time.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.description: The temporal and spatial distribution of parameters and activations across the hardware's memory hierarchy.

concepts.name: Dataflow Optimization
concepts.description: The strategic scheduling of data movement through hardware to maximize operand reuse and minimize expensive external memory accesses.

concepts.name: Operator Fusion
concepts.description: The optimization technique of combining multiple adjacent operations into a single kernel execution to eliminate intermediate memory round-trips.

concepts.name: Weight Stationary
concepts.description: A dataflow strategy that keeps model parameters fixed in local memory while streaming inputs and accumulating partial sums.

concepts.name: Output Stationary
concepts.description: A dataflow strategy that keeps intermediate partial sums fixed in local memory to eliminate redundant memory write operations.

concepts.name: Input Stationary
concepts.description: A dataflow strategy that keeps input activations fixed in local memory while streaming weights and partial sums through the processing elements.
